title: Codex Help-Claude Response
description: Structured response when Claude is stuck on a task and asks Codex for help. One of these per round of a codex-help loop.

analysis.description: Codex's read on what Claude is actually facing. One paragraph. State what the blocker is, the constraints Codex is treating as given, and which parts are inference.

diagnosis.description: The key finding — root cause, missing insight, or reframing Claude needed. One sentence to one short paragraph. This is the 'aha' for Claude to act on.

next_steps.description: Concrete actions Claude should take, in order. Each is actionable from Claude's seat — specific files, commands, or edits, not vague directives.

action.description: What Claude should do. Imperative and specific — e.g. 'Read lib/foo.ts:42-67 to see how X handles Y', not 'investigate lib/foo.ts'.

why.description: How this step moves Claude forward. Terse.

missing_context.description: Things Codex could not verify from Claude's summary + repo state. If non-empty, Claude should gather these before committing to the next_steps. Empty array is fine when Codex had enough context.

open_questions.description: Questions Codex would want answered (possibly by the user) before committing harder to the diagnosis. Empty array is fine.
